stories.title: CPSC Recall — Cosyland Children's Tower Stools (125,200 Units)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: 125,200 Cosyland tower stools pulled by CPSC.

Platform can loosen, tip over. Entrapment risk. 8 injuries reported.

Stop use now. Free repair kit: 866-677-3889 or cosyland.com/pages/recall

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Bought a Cosyland tower stool on Amazon (April 2021–Nov 2025)?

Check model number: CS0003 or CS0092-4 = recalled. Platform loosens over time — fall and entrapment hazard.

Free fix: cosyland.com/pages/recall

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your family uses a Cosyland children's tower stool, stop using it now. 🚨

The CPSC announced on May 14 that 125,200 Cosyland tower stools — sold on Amazon from April 2021 through November 2025 for about $70 — have been recalled due to fall and entrapment hazards. The recalled models are CS0003 and CS0092-4 (natural bamboo and gray, about 35 inches tall).

Here's what's happening: the platform inside can loosen over time, becoming unstable or detaching entirely. That's a fall hazard. And because a child's torso can fit through openings on the front and back sides of the stool, there's also a serious entrapment risk if the unit tips. The firm has received 25 incident reports, including 8 injuries ranging from scrapes to a fractured arm.

The good news is that there's a free fix. Contact Cosyland at 866-677-3889 (Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM PT) or go to cosyland.com/pages/recall. They'll mail you a repair kit — protective net, stabilizing feet, and installation instructions — at no cost. In the meantime, store the stool away from your kids.

If you're not sure whether your stool is one of the recalled models, check the model number (CS0003 or CS0092-4) on the underside of the unit.

Has your family used one of these stools? Have you already gotten the recall notice?
facebook.image_caption: RECALL: 125,200 Cosyland children's tower stools recalled. Stop use immediately. Free repair kit at cosyland.com/pages/recall or 866-677-3889. Models CS0003 and CS0092-4. 🚨

article.headline: CPSC Recall: 125,200 Cosyland Children's Tower Stools — Stop Use Now

stories.title: D11 Labor Dispute — 300+ Days Since Master Contract Ended, Families Still Feeling the Impact

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
D11 ended its 50-year master contract with teachers in June 2025.

300+ days later: educators picketed the Board, teachers went on strike.

School year ends May 22. What does fall 2026 look like?

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
D11 school year ends May 22.

Teachers have worked without a master contract for 300+ days. The union struck in October. The board replaced it with a handbook.

What does this mean for teacher retention next fall?

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
As D11 families count down to the last day of school on May 22, it's worth pausing on what's been happening behind the scenes this year — and what it could mean for fall.

In June 2025, the D11 Board of Education voted 7-1 to allow the district's master agreement with the Colorado Springs Education Association (CSEA) to expire. That agreement had been in place for more than 50 years. In its place, D11 introduced an employee handbook — which the union argues doesn't carry the same legal standing as a collectively bargained contract.

Teachers went on strike in October 2025. In early May 2026, educators and parents came back out to picket a D11 Board meeting, marking more than 300 days since the agreement ended. The union's argument has been consistent: without a binding contract, teachers have less job security, and that affects who stays in our schools.

The district has pushed back, saying it's focused on progress and rejects what it calls misinformation being spread by union organizers. Both sides have their view, and COS families are caught in the middle — watching teachers they know and trust navigate uncertainty as the school year closes.

Whether you're a family in D11, D20, or D49, this situation raises real questions: Is your school keeping good teachers? Are there open positions going into next year? It's worth asking.

What are you hearing from teachers at your school — and what questions do you have about next fall?
facebook.image_caption: D11's master contract with teachers ended June 2025. 300+ days later, educators and parents are still raising concerns. School year ends May 22 — and fall 2026 is right around the corner. 🏫

article.headline: D11's Labor Dispute at 300 Days: What COS Families Need to Know

stories.title: Territory Days 2026 — 50th Anniversary, Free, Memorial Day Weekend

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Territory Days turns 50 this year — May 23–25 in Old Colorado City.

Free admission. 180+ booths. Petting zoo. Toy trains. Gunfight re-enactments.

COS's best free Memorial Day weekend tradition.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Heading to Territory Days (May 23–25)? Parking tip:

C.A.B. Garage, 130 S. Nevada Ave — $1/hr. Free shuttle to Colorado Avenue.

50th anniversary. Free. Memorial Day weekend. Old Colorado City.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Mark your calendars: Territory Days is back May 23–25 in Old Colorado City, and this year is the 50th anniversary. 🎪

It's one of the best free family weekends in Colorado Springs, and the lineup proves it: 180+ food and craft booths, live music from more than 25 bands across multiple stages, a petting zoo, toy train rides, mechanical bull, gold panning, live birds of prey, Indigenous dancers, Wild West gunfight re-enactments, and a dedicated kids' play area. Memorial Day weekend and completely free admission.

The festival runs along Colorado Avenue between 23rd and 27th Street in the heart of Old Colorado City. Parking is easy: head to the C.A.B. Parking Garage at 130 S. Nevada Ave downtown — it's $1 per hour — and hop on the free shuttle buses that run to the festival all weekend.

A few tips for families: mornings are typically cooler and less crowded if you've got young kids. Afternoons bring more music and the full vendor market energy. The petting zoo and kids' area tend to be busiest midday Saturday.

Are you making Territory Days part of your Memorial Day weekend plans this year?
facebook.image_caption: Territory Days is May 23–25 in Old Colorado City — and it's the 50th anniversary. Free admission, 180+ booths, petting zoo, live music, and a kids' play area. One of COS's best free family events of the year. 🎪

article.headline: Territory Days 2026: COS's 50th Annual Free Family Festival Is Memorial Day Weekend

stories.title: CPSC Recall — BabyBond Retractable Safety Gates (280 Units)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
RECALL: BabyBond retractable safety gates (model SH20.006DB) recalled.

Sold at Target.com Nov 2024–Oct 2025, ~$50. Child's torso fits through floor gap — entrapment risk.

Return to any Target for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you bought a BabyBond retractable mesh safety gate from Target in the last year or two, check it right now.

The CPSC has announced a recall of approximately 280 BabyBond retractable safety gates (model number SH20.006DB) due to a serious entrapment hazard. The issue: a child's torso can fit through the gap between the bottom of the gate and the floor. That gap shouldn't exist — and it puts young kids at risk of becoming stuck, which can cause serious injury or death.

The gates were sold in black, gray, and white on Target.com from November 2024 through October 2025 for about $50. They have a metal frame and adjust in width up to 55 inches.

Here's what to do: stop using the gate immediately and return it to any Target store for a full refund. No questions asked. You can also contact the company directly at 844-406-3569 (Monday through Friday, 10 AM to 4 PM PT) or by email at elsie@BabyBond-global.com.

The model number is on a white label stitched to the mesh fabric on the side of the gate — if it says SH20.006DB, that's the one.

Did you buy one of these? Worth a quick check.
facebook.image_caption: RECALL: BabyBond retractable safety gates recalled (model SH20.006DB, sold at Target.com). Stop use immediately. Return to any Target store for a full refund. 🚨 Contact: 844-406-3569.

article.headline: CPSC Recall: BabyBond Retractable Safety Gates Sold at Target — Return for Full Refund

stories.title: Your COS Summer Camp Guide 2026 — What's Open and How to Register

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
D11 wraps up May 22. Summer plans locked in?

COS has theater, outdoor adventure, golf, art, and multi-sport camps for ages 4–18.

Search coloradospringskids.com for the full 2026 camp directory.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D11 wraps up on May 22. Have your summer plans locked in? 🏕️

Colorado Springs has a genuinely strong lineup of summer camps this year — and a few of them are filling up fast. Here's a quick look at what's available across different categories:

**Theater:** CYT Colorado Springs has Shrek Jr. and Willy Wonka Kids camps for ages 5–13. Theater camps tend to fill quickly because the cast sizes are limited.

**Outdoor Adventure:** Go West Camps offers day camp and teen adventure programs for ages 5–14. COS's mountain access makes outdoor programs especially good here.

**Sports:** First Tee Southern Colorado is running golf camps that combine instruction with life skills for kids. Life Time COS has multi-sport camps for ages 4–13.

**Art:** Peaks & Paint is an art studio camp for ages 6–13 — painting, drawing, and pottery in a real studio environment.

**Multi-Activity:** Steve & Kate's Camp sells a summer pass covering up to 53 days of camp for one fee, which can be great for families who need flexible coverage.

The most comprehensive directory for COS camps is at coloradospringskids.com/summer-camp-directory. Macaroni KID Colorado Springs also has a 2026 guide at coloradosprings.macaronikid.com.

What kind of camp has worked best for your family — and are there any that you'd recommend to other COS parents?
facebook.image_caption: D11 ends May 22. Summer camp season is here. COS has theater, sports, outdoor adventure, art, and STEM camps for ages 4–18. Full guide at coloradospringskids.com/summer-camp-directory. 🏕️

article.headline: COS Summer Camp Guide 2026: Registration Is Open and Spots Are Filling
